solutions.solution: According to the question, the direction diagram is as follows [diagram showing path with coordinates]. PQ = TR = 2m, QR = PT = 3m. RS = 6m, TS = RS - TR = 6 - 2 = 4m. Required distance, PS = √(PT)² + (TS)² = √(3)² + (4)² = √9 + 16 = √25 = 5m

According to the question, the direction diagram is as follows [diagram showing triangular path]. AO = OB, OC = OD = 3km. AC = BD = 4km, AO = √(4)² + (3)² = √16 + 9 = √25 = 5km. AO = OB. Required distance, AB = AO + OB = AO + AO = 5 + 5 = 10km

According to the question, the direction diagram is as follows [diagram showing rectangular path]. A = Starting point, E = Finishing point. AB = 3km, CD = EB = 6km, BC = DE = 5km. Required distance, AE = EB - AB = 6 - 3 = 3km

According to the question, the direction diagram will be as follows [diagram]. RQ = QC = 8/2 = 4m as Q is the mid-point between R and C. GS = SH = 6/2 = 3m as S is the mid-point between G and H. RG = 8m, QK = 10m, GH = 6m, QL = 3m

Clearly, the person starts from L goes 4m southward and reaches point S from where he takes right turn and walks 3m to reach G. [diagram] Clearly, man will cross L and at the final point, he will be at a distance of 3m from R.
According to the question, the direction diagram will be as follows [diagram showing path]. O = Mid-point of BC and AD. AB = CD = 4km, BC = 6km
